calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report
Off Calendar Jul-22-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Richard Sax appeared by zoom for William Chappell (Administrator).

The Court stated that it was its understanding that the estate was opened for the purpose of closing the Exeter property sale and that Peggie initiated the sale before she died. Mr. Sax stated that Peggie helped with her credit, that they had an agreement to sell the property to Edward Goode, and that they filed for heggstad in Sonoma County, but it was denied. Mr. Sax also stated that they had extraordinary problems with the title company, that the title company lost original documents, that the title officer quit and that they had all kinds of problems. In addition, Mr. Sax stated that $220,000.00 went to the bank, that it was not noted as such because that's the way the title company did their closing statement, and that Mr. Chappell declares under penalty and perjury that it is no income to the estate. Furthermore, Mr. Sax stated that Edward is the purchaser of the property and a friend to Peggie, that there was a trust and that his recollection is that Peggie was a resident in San Francisco when she died. The First and Final Account and Report of Administrator, etc. is under submission.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: D. Johnson. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report

Petition For Approval Of Updated Status Report Of Executor, William W. Chappell
Off Calendar Nov-02-2020 Granted Per Written Order

Petition For Orders: 1) Nunc Pro Tunc Order To Amend Title Of Proceedings; And 2) Amendment To Order On Verified Petition Filed On April 25, 2018
Off Calendar Aug-03-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Richard Sax appeared by zoom for William Chappell (Personal Representative).

The Court stated that it looks like the parties ran into some problems based on the aka's (also known as) of Ms. Chappell, that some documents show "Peggy" as the spelling of the first and the estate was opened with "Peggie", and noted that the notice to administer the estate and publication at the outset included all of the aka's. The Court also informed Counsel that the request by the title company to include additional relief regarding the option papers goes beyond the realm nunc pro tunc. The Petition for Nunc Pro Tunc Order Amending Title of Proceedings and Order Confirming Sale of Real Property is granted in part and denied in part. The Court will not direct filing of a copy of the option papers.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Probate Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Deputy: K. Cabasares. Not reported.
